Converter application flexibility — optimised per use case
Why one optimisation target doesn't fit all
Different applications have fundamentally different load profiles. The same converter hardware can be tuned for any of these by selecting the appropriate optimisation target.
⚡ Electric Vehicles — 4 kW optimised
Operate near full-load continuously. Dead time tuned for peak efficiency at 4 kW (98.88%). Accepts lower efficiency at light load.
🔋 Energy Storage — 1 kW optimised
Primarily light-to-medium load operation. 1 kW optimisation maximises efficiency in the 500W–2kW range (97.2% peak at 2kW).
☀ Solar PV — CEC weighted
Evaluated using CEC standard (53% weight at 75% load). CEC optimisation delivers balanced efficiency across the full range — CEC η=96.89%.
How each efficiency target is calculated — fmincon algorithm

Future work — cost as second objective
This work optimises efficiency only. The next step (planned publication) extends fmincon to a true bi-objective problem — simultaneously maximising efficiency and minimising component cost across all switch technology combinations (GaN-GaN, SiC-Si, Si-Si). The Pareto front reveals that Si-Si combinations at normalised cost 0.15–0.20 can reach 96–97% — nearly matching GaN at a fraction of the cost. Future work will also include EMI constraints, packaging parasitics, ML-based adaptive control, and multi-port (3-port) DAB for renewable energy.
